2605.14665.title: Falkor-IRAC: Graph-Constrained Generation for Verified Legal Reasoning in Indian Judicial AI
2605.14665.paper_summary: Legal reasoning is not semantic similarity search. A court judgment encodes constrained symbolic reasoning: precedent propagation, procedural state transitions, and statute-bound inference. These are properties that vector-based retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) cannot faithfully represent. Hallucinated precedents, outdated statute citations, and unsupported reasoning chains remain persistent failure modes in LLM-based legal AI, with real consequences for access to justice in high-caseload jurisdictions such as India. This paper presents Falkor-IRAC, a graph-constrained generation framework for Indian legal AI that grounds generation in structured reasoning over an IRAC (Issue, Rule, Analysis, Conclusion) knowledge graph. Judgments from the Supreme Court and High Courts of India are ingested as IRAC node structures enriched with procedural state transitions, precedent relationships, and statutory references, stored in FalkorDB for low-latency agentic traversal. At inference time, LLM-generated answers are accepted only if a valid supporting path can be traced through the graph, a check performed by a falsifiability oracle called the Verifier Agent. The system also detects doctrinal conflicts as a first-class output rather than silently resolving them. Falkor-IRAC is evaluated using graph-native metrics: citation grounding accuracy, path validity rate, hallucinated precedent rate, and conflict detection rate. These metrics are argued to be more appropriate for legal reasoning evaluation than BLEU and ROUGE. On a proof-of-concept corpus of 51 Supreme Court judgments, the Verifier Agent correctly validated citations on completed queries and correctly rejected fabricated citations. Evaluation against vector-only RAG baselines is left for future work, as is GPU-accelerated inference to address current timeout rates on CPU hardware.
2605.14665.paper_summary_zh: 法律推理並不是語意相似性搜索。法院判決編碼了受限的符號推理：先例傳播、程序狀態轉換和法規約束的推理。這些是基於向量的檢索增強生成（RAG）無法忠實表現的特性。幻覺的先例、過時的法規引用和不支持的推理鏈在基於大型語言模型（LLM）的法律人工智慧中仍然是持續的失敗模式，對於像印度這樣的高案件負擔司法管轄區的司法獲取產生了實際後果。本文提出了Falkor-IRAC，一個針對印度法律人工智慧的圖約束生成框架，將生成基於對IRAC（問題、規則、分析、結論）知識圖的結構化推理。來自印度最高法院和高等法院的判決被作為IRAC節點結構引入，並增強了程序狀態轉換、先例關係和法規引用，存儲在FalkorDB中以便於低延遲的代理遍歷。在推理時，僅當能夠通過圖追溯到有效的支持路徑時，LLM生成的答案才會被接受，這一檢查由一個稱為驗證代理的可證偽性神諭執行。該系統還將教義衝突檢測為一級輸出，而不是默默解決它們。Falkor-IRAC使用圖原生指標進行評估：引用基礎準確率、路徑有效性率、幻覺先例率和衝突檢測率。這些指標被認為比BLEU和ROUGE更適合法律推理評估。在51個最高法院判決的概念驗證語料庫上，驗證代理正確驗證了已完成查詢的引用，並正確拒絕了虛構的引用。針對僅基於向量的RAG基準的評估留待未來工作，GPU加速推理以解決當前CPU硬體上的超時率也是如此。
2605.14665.author: Joy Bose et.al.
